items.title: Your data built the AI boom — but Big Tech is pocketing 100% of the equity
items.description: Your share of the AI wealth is a right — not a handout. Here is how we claw back our money.

items.title: Only 5% of U.S. adults can ace this 8-question financial-literacy test. Can you?
items.description: Financial literacy just hit a 10-year low — and it is devastating bank accounts.

items.title: You want a portfolio that matches your morals. Your retirement plan might disagree.
items.description: More investors want their money to reflect their values. It feels righteous — but the reality is a lot messier.

items.title: The dot-com crash was a $5 trillion blip. Why the next financial crisis could hit 4 times harder.
items.description: The next global financial crisis is already under way. Here is how it will unfold.

items.title: Stocks rally when Congress goes on summer break. Here’s why.
items.description: Stock prices face greater volatility when lawmakers are active, driven entirely by the regulatory uncertainty they create.

items.title: Your investing expectations are more than double the reality. Here is the tough truth.
items.description: Long-term real returns above 10% annualized are exceedingly rare.

items.title: Corporate America is pumping $1 trillion into stock buybacks — but look at what insiders are doing
items.description: These 22 U.S. stocks are backed by company buybacks and insider cash.

items.title: ‘I’d hate to end up with an unexpected tax bill’: I’m 73 and still work full time. Can I avoid paying taxes on my Social Security benefits?
items.description: “I’m actually earning more each week than I ever have before.”

items.title: The U.S. stock market is becoming ‘too big to fail’
items.description: Why protracted bear markets may be a thing of the past.

items.title: ‘An American tragedy’ in the making: The majority of small-business owners will retire in the next decade. What happens to their workers?
items.description: “It’s a loss to the economy, to jobs, to decades of institutional knowledge when a business simply turns off the lights”
